learning: Built end-to-end privacy/legal tooling: added Sanity schema for privacy and Google Ads guidance, created a publisher script plus supporting ads-guidelines checklist, and wired new /privacy-policy and /ads-guidance routes with a legal data helper so the front-end can show those pages once the CMS document exists.
mistakes: Initially no Sanity credentials (so the policy looked empty), the Netlify build failed because whisper.cpp wasn’t excluded from TypeScript scanning, and I kept trying to push without a stored PAT which led to repeated 403 permission errors until the token was reintroduced temporarily.
highlights: Updated the site repo, published the privacy policy via npm script, regenerated the lockfile for Node 22, excluded whisper.cpp in tsconfig, and got npm run build/Netlify deploys working (after clearing cache) while pushing all changes to origin/main.
decisions: Keep manifest of Sanity creds in .env.sanity for future publishes, continue using PAT or SSH for git pushes but delete tokens after use, and rely on the new publisher script + ads guidance page for monetization transparency.
extras: Future work: seed the Google Ads guidance document as well, add footer links to the new policy/guidance pages, and optionally clear Netlify caches when dependency issues recur.
cleanedConversation:
- Added privacy/legal schema, publisher script, and front-end routes for policy/guidance.
- Fixed Netlify build by clearing cache and excluding whisper.cpp from TypeScript.
- Handled PAT push failures (temporal storage) and considered SSH setup.
- Ran npm run publish-privacy-policy once .env.sanity was present so the policy doc exists in Sanity.
- Final user request: summarize and store this conversation for future tracking.
